subject: Update defconfig (enable logitech wheels).
author: Sorgelig

upstream.vanilla_quote:
config LOGIG940_FF
	bool "Logitech Flight System G940 force feedback support"
	depends on HID_LOGITECH
	select INPUT_FF_MEMLESS
---
config LOGIWHEELS_FF
	bool "Logitech wheels configuration and force feedback support"
	depends on HID_LOGITECH
	select INPUT_FF_MEMLESS
	default LOGITECH_FF

provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md: grouped row 'defconfig (11 commits)' — disposition: 'config — feeds P1.3'

impact.effect_if_absent: Logitech G940 flight stick and Logitech wheels (G25/G27/G29/Driving Force series) would lose force feedback support

superseded_by: board/mister/de10nano/linux.config (CONFIG_LOGIG940_FF=y explicit; CONFIG_LOGIWHEELS_FF achieved via Kconfig default=LOGITECH_FF resolution, confirmed in the actual resolved build config)

notes: Pure kconfig change (defconfig update, not a code patch). Both CONFIG_LOGIG940_FF and CONFIG_LOGIWHEELS_FF exist as standard vanilla options in Linux 6.18 (drivers/hid/Kconfig:697, 705). Current status in our linux.config: CONFIG_LOGIG940_FF=y (explicit); CONFIG_LOGIWHEELS_FF not listed but defaults to CONFIG_LOGITECH_FF (which is y in our config). Commit's intent (enable both) is achieved via current linux.config. Stock reference (stock-linux.config) confirms both should be y. This commit is listed in patch-provenance.md as part of a grouped row of 11 defconfig commits ('config — feeds P1.3'), indicating it was grouped in prior analysis. No patch carried (defconfig changes are config, not patches). | sonnet-verified: DISPOSITION CORRECTED not-evaluated -> dropped-deliberate. The 'default LOGITECH_FF' claim is confirmed at drivers/hid/Kconfig (vanilla 6.18): 'config LOGIWHEELS_FF ... default LOGITECH_FF'; LOGIG940_FF has no default line (defaults n) but IS explicit =y in our fragment. Crucially this was verified against the STRONGEST possible evidence, not just static Kconfig reading: the actual post-olddefconfig resolved kernel .config at /mnt/source/Buildroot_MiSTer/output/build/linux-6.18.38/.config shows CONFIG_LOGITECH_FF=y, CONFIG_LOGIG940_FF=y, AND CONFIG_LOGIWHEELS_FF=y (lines 2949, 2951, 2952) even though LOGIWHEELS_FF has no line at all in our board/mister/de10nano/linux.config fragment (grep confirms absent) — proving the default-resolution chain actually fires in our real build, not just in theory. Per the macvlan-pilot precedent (not-evaluated is reserved for cases where OUR build genuinely lacks the resulting symbol, e.g. CONFIG_MACVLAN which has no line anywhere and resolves to unset), this record's prior 'not-evaluated' was a misapplication: the commit's intent (both wheel/flight-stick FF options enabled) IS achieved in our actual build. Corrected to dropped-deliberate, superseded_by updated to cite the default-resolution mechanism, agrees_with_provenance_doc set true. No userspace_coupling citation was asserted (coupled=false stands, unchanged).
